name: Layout utilities
description: Display, positioning scheme, flexbox alignment/direction, and the visually-hidden accessibility pattern.

families.name: visually-hidden
families.description: Hides content visually while keeping it available to assistive technology.
guidance: Use for screen-reader-only text (icon button labels, skip links at rest, table caption context).
Never use on focusable elements unless focus reveals them — a focused but invisible element strands keyboard users.
families.a11y: Core accessibility primitive: content stays in the accessibility tree.

families.name: position
families.description: Sets the positioning scheme of an element.
guidance: Pair position-absolute/fixed/sticky with offset utilities (top-*, left-*) from the position partial.
Prefer position-sticky over position-fixed for in-flow pinned headers — it reserves layout space.

families.name: display
families.description: Sets the display box type of an element.
guidance: display-none removes content from the accessibility tree; use visually-hidden when screen readers should still announce it.

families.name: flex-direction
families.description: Sets the main axis direction of a flex container.

families.name: align-items
families.description: Aligns flex children on the cross axis.

families.name: align-self
families.description: Overrides the container's align-items for a single flex child.

families.name: justify-content
families.description: Distributes flex children along the main axis.
